summary: Finished decompiling the mnhyaku menu module by deleting asm/melee/mn/mnhyaku.s, switching mnhyaku.c to Matching in configure.py and obj_files.mk, adding a real mnhyaku.h, and renaming local fn_ symbols at 8024C9F0/8024CA50/8024CAC8 to mnHyaku_* in symbols.txt and C. The source changes are mostly matching-driven refactors: stack padding, explicit control-flow, corrected signatures/types, HSD_JObj casts, a small inline menu allocation helper, and build/symbol cleanup.

key_files.role: Removed 628-line assembly fallback for mnhyaku after source became matching.
Updated C implementation with matching-oriented control flow, prototypes, casts, stack padding, static data, helper extraction, and renamed local functions.
Added header guard, platform/baselib includes, and public prototypes for mnhyaku functions.
Renamed fn_8024C9F0, fn_8024CA50, and fn_8024CAC8 to mnHyaku_8024C9F0, mnHyaku_8024CA50, and mnHyaku_8024CAC8.
Switched text object from asm/melee/mn/mnhyaku.s.o to src/melee/mn/mnhyaku.c.o.

what_changed: Deleted the mnhyaku assembly file and made the C object the linked text object.
Marked melee/mn/mnhyaku.c as Matching in configure.py.

Added src/melee/mn/mnhyaku.h with header guard _MN_HYAKU_, platform/baselib includes, and prototypes for mnHyaku_8024C68C, mnHyaku_8024C9F0, mnHyaku_8024CA50, mnHyaku_8024CAC8, mnHyaku_8024CB94, and mnHyaku_8024CD64.
Moved direct baselib/platform includes and local prototypes out of mnhyaku.c and into the new header, then included "mnhyaku.h" from the C file.
Changed mnHyaku_804D6C58 from extern to static in mnhyaku.c.
Renamed previously anonymous/local symbols fn_8024C9F0, fn_8024CA50, and fn_8024CAC8 to mnHyaku-prefixed names in both symbols.txt and function pointer usage.
Adjusted mnHyaku_8024C68C matching details: added PAD_STACK(16), tested input with ret & 0x10 instead of if (ret), and expanded cursor wrap ternaries into explicit if/else branches.
Adjusted HSD object typing by introducing HSD_JObj* locals and casting gobj->hsd_obj before animation helper calls.
Added PAD_STACK(12) and clearer locals in mnHyaku_8024CAC8, including a stored f32 result from mn_8022EC18 before comparing against mnHyaku_803EF668[1].
Added inline static allocMenu(u8 arg0, HSD_GObj* gobj) to allocate 8 bytes, report/assert on failure, initialize cursor/text, and attach user data with GObj_InitUserData.
Changed lbAudioAx_8002702C declaration and call shape from three s32 arguments to (s32, u64), with the call becoming lbAudioAx_8002702C(2, 8).
Removed an unused Vec3 construction in mnHyaku_8024CD64 while preserving the lbAudioAx_80027648() call.
smart_moves: Finishing the module included the full build pipeline update: remove asm, switch obj_files.mk to the C object, and mark the object Matching in configure.py.
Renaming fn_ symbols to mnHyaku_* avoided leaving completed code with generic local names and made symbols searchable by module prefix.
The new header concentrated platform/baselib includes and prototypes, reducing ad-hoc declarations in the C file.
Using explicit if/else cursor wrap logic instead of ternaries is a common decomp matching tactic when the compiler needs branch structure matching the original assembly.
Adding PAD_STACK at exact functions indicates the match required artificial stack layout preservation rather than semantic changes.
Changing `if (ret)` to `if (ret & 0x10)` preserved the intended controller bitmask test after earlier button cases, matching the original assembly's explicit mask.
The inline allocation helper kept user-data allocation and initialization compact while still matching allocation/assert behavior.
decomp_lessons: When completing a module, do not stop at source matching: remove the asm file, switch obj_files.mk from asm to src, and update configure.py to Matching in the same PR.
Generic `fn_` symbols should be renamed to the module prefix once their owning module is known; update symbols.txt and all C function pointer references together.
Controller input routines often need exact bitmask conditions, not broad truthiness checks, especially when multiple buttons are tested sequentially.
Small source-level style differences such as ternary cursor wrapping versus explicit branches can affect matching.
Keep calls with side effects even if their return value is unused; this PR removed a temporary Vec3 but retained lbAudioAx_80027648().
Header extraction can be part of finishing a module: move baselib type includes and prototypes into a module header so source files avoid local forward declarations.
Type the HSD object payload explicitly when matching animation code; using HSD_JObj* locals and casts can influence register allocation and call sequences.
Stack padding macros are acceptable targeted tools when the decompiled logic is correct but the compiler stack frame still differs.
naming_conventions: Module functions use the `mnHyaku_` prefix followed by the address, e.g. mnHyaku_8024C9F0, mnHyaku_8024CA50, mnHyaku_8024CAC8.
Avoid leaving known module functions named `fn_8024....` after ownership is established.
The new header uses the guard `_MN_HYAKU_`.
The file name and module path use lowercase `mnhyaku`, while symbols use camel-style `mnHyaku`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the 628-line asm fallback after mnhyaku.c matched.
Inserted PAD_STACK(16) in mnHyaku_8024C68C and PAD_STACK(12) in mnHyaku_8024CAC8.
Expanded ternary cursor updates into explicit if/else to match branch layout.
Used `ret & 0x10`, `ret & 4`, and `ret & 8` style button-bit tests rather than broad truthiness.
Introduced HSD_JObj* locals and casts from gobj->hsd_obj to shape code generation around animation calls.
Stored HSD_GObjProc* results in locals named proc/gobj_proc before assigning flags_3.
Used an inline static allocMenu helper for allocation, assert, initialization, and GObj_InitUserData behavior.
Adjusted the lbAudioAx_8002702C prototype to a two-argument `(s32, u64)` form to match the call/codegen.
Removed unused local Vec3 setup while retaining the function call whose return had previously populated it.
follow_up_queries: Find other mn modules still using fn_ symbols near completed source files.
Search for NonMatching menu modules in configure.py that still link asm in obj_files.mk.
Look for controller input handlers using broad `if (ret)` checks where explicit bitmasks may be needed.
Search for menu modules with local prototypes/includes that should be moved into module headers.
